text: This week, over 120 AI updates dropped across the industry. But here are the top 10 you shouldn't miss. Anthropic just turned Claude into Microsoft Office's worst nightmare. On September 9th, they announced that Claude can now create actual files, Excel spreadsheets with working formulas, PowerPoint presentations, Word documents, PDFs, not just instructions, but real files you can download and use. Now, you can simply say, "Create a financial model for my food truck business." and Claude will build an actual Excel file with multiple sheets, formulas, charts, everything. You can download it, open it in Excel, and it works perfectly. Simon Willis from the Python Software Foundation called it saying, "Talk about burying the lead. This is their version of chat GPT code interpreter. My all-time favorite feature. One food truck owner uploaded messy sales data and said, "Turn this into something my investors will understand." Claude cleaned everything up, created charts, and even built a professional PowerPoint with insights. minutes, not hours. How does it work? Claude now has access to a private computer environment where it can write code and run programs to create the files you need. Think of it like Claude having its own computer to actually build your documents instead of just telling you how. Anthropic revenue hit $2 billion this year, and that's before this feature. It's available now for Macs, team, and enterprise users with pro users getting it soon. There is one catch. This feature gives Claude internet access to create and analyze files which could potentially put your data at risk. Anthropic advises watching your chats closely since malicious files could trick Claude into doing things you don't want. To try it, go to settings, click on features, then experimental and turn on upgraded file creation and analysis. Upload your data, tell Claude what you need, and get real files back instantly. The gap between idea and execution is shrinking. Your AI assistant just became your actual assistant. No more copypaste marathons. Microsoft Office suddenly looking very outdated. Cling AI just made digital humans indistinguishable from real ones. With their avatar feature, you can take any image, whether it's you, a cartoon, or a historical photo, and bring it to life with realistic emotions and expressions. Here's how it works. Upload your avatar image and audio. Then prompt the AI with the emotions you want. Choose happy, sad, surprised, and the AI doesn't just move lips. It transforms a static image into something fluid and alive. Powered by Cling 1.6, which is 195% more powerful than the previous version, it delivers the most realistic movements and lifelike expressions. This model can now animate human subjects or characters with incredible detail. Creators are using this tech for virtual spokespeople in presentations, social media, and educational videos. Gaming companies are bringing NPCs to life without needing motion capture. This is all possible because Cling uses deep learning based 3D modeling which reconstructs facial expressions and body postures from 2D images, making the

This is all possible because Cling uses deep learning based 3D modeling which reconstructs facial expressions and body postures from 2D images, making the feature is in limited access, but you can get early access. Full global access is coming soon. By adding emotional depth to avatars, clink could change everything from Hollywood to Tik Tok. Oracle just had the most insane day in its 47-year history. The stock shot up 36% adding a jaw-dropping $244 billion in value in just one day. That's Oracle's biggest daily gain since 1992. And it sent Larry Ellison to the top of the billionaire rankings. Ellison is now the world's richest man. So, what triggered this massive search? Oracle announced their cloud infrastructure backlog hit $455 billion. That's future revenue that's already locked in. They're also projecting $18 billion in cloud revenue next year. Analysts are loving it. Deutsche Bank called Oracle's results truly awesome. And Wells Fargo said it was a momentous confirmation of the AI trade. But here's where it gets personal. Larry Ellison owns 41% of Oracle, which means he has about 1.16 billion shares. When the stock surged, his net worth jumped by over 100 billion in a single day. For a few hours, he was actually richer than Elon Musk with a net worth of $393 billion compared to Musk's $385 billion. Now, here's the catch that nobody's talking about. The Wall Street Journal reported that Oracle signed a $300 billion 5-year deal with OpenAI. Most of Oracle's rosy outlook is tied to one major unprofitable AI company. Saffracats, Oracle CEO, said they've signed huge cloud contracts with the who's who of AI, including OpenAI, XAI, and Meta. But remember, OpenAI is burning through billions each year. So, while this looks great on paper, there's a big risk involved. Oracle is betting everything on AI infrastructure. They've raised their capital expenditures to $35 billion this year, building data centers at a breakneck pace to meet skyrocketing demand. Either Larry Ellison just made the trade of the century, or we're watching history repeat itself. Time will tell. Genspark just dropped the world's first AI browser with 169 AI models running entirely on your device. That's right, no internet needed. Your data stays on your machine. And the best part, it's completely free. So what does it do? You get to choose from 169 openweight models like GPT, DeepSeek, Gemma, and more. All running locally, meaning you get instant responses with no lag. Forget waiting on servers, API limits, or subscription fees. Here's where it gets wild. Autopilot mode. Imagine you want to research the best laptop under $1,500. Just hit autopilot and the browser takes control. It opens tabs, compares prices, and gives you a summary without you lifting a finger. fees. Here's where it gets wild. Autopilot mode. Imagine you want to research the best laptop under $1,500. Just hit autopilot and the browser takes control. It opens tabs, compares prices, and gives you a summary without you lifting a finger. Then there are the super agents, your intelligent browsing deals, compare products, analyze reviews, and make informed decisions on any website. On Amazon, it can search for lower prices. On YouTube, it can summarize content or even extract transcripts for you, all without you having to watch the video. The MCP store connects 700 plus tools like Discord, GitHub, Notion, Slack, and more. Now, let's talk performance. In just 45 days, Ginspark hit $36 million in annual recurring revenue with just a 20 person team. No paid ads, all organic growth driven by the product's virality. And here's a nice touch. Ad blocking is on by default. So, no annoying banners or pop-ups slow you down. just a lightning fast, clean web experience. Suddenly, Chrome and Edge look like calculators next to a supercomput. A Chinese startup just shook things up. They built an AI that's smarter than GPT5 and gave it away for free. If you're paying $20 a month for Chad GPT plus, you're getting beat. Moonshot AI just released Kimmy K2 and it outperforms GPT5 Claude and Gemini across math, coding, and reasoning all for free. But here's the real twist. OpenAI charges you $15 per million words processed. Kim K2 just $2.50 per million. Or better yet, download it yourself and pay nothing forever. The best part, the CEO of Moonshot AI, Yang Jillin, isn't just any founder. He wrote the research papers OpenAI used to create GPT. Now, he's using that knowledge to challenge their monopoly. And with 1.3 billion backing from Alibaba and Tencent, he's got the resources to make it happen. You've got three ways to access it. Use their website just like you would chat GPT. Pay 90% less for their API or simply download it and run it completely free on your own system. This is a gamecher. Startups now have access to enterprise level AI without the huge cost. In a world where AI giants charge premium prices, Moonshot AI is giving it away and it's about to flip the whole industry upside down. Elon Musk just turned Grock into a video studio and it's making waves. Have you heard of Grock? Imagine it creates 6-second animated videos from text or voice prompts and must calls it AI vine but with a twist that has everyone talking. So why is this such a big deal? Most AI video tools are stuck with safe sanitized content and strict rules. Grock imagine is different. No waiting for weeks, no spending thousands. You type something like cyberpunk city with flying cars and within 30 seconds Grock delivers cinema quality footage with ambient sounds. How wild is that? One user asked for an astronaut on an alien landscape, and Grock generated perfectly

You type something like cyberpunk city with flying cars and within 30 seconds Grock delivers cinema quality footage with ambient sounds. How wild is that? While other platforms have strict guidelines, Grock lets you create more creative content with fewer restrictions. Users are pushing the limits and creating things that would be difficult on other platforms, and creators are having a blast experimenting. The tech behind it, 110,000 Nvidia GPUs in Xi's Memphis Supercomputer running the Aurora engine. Musk's not just competing, he's rewriting the rules. When users compared Grock to Google's V3, Musk said, "It's all about the fastest time to make a fun, sharable video. You can create videos and post them directly to X. It's available for Super Grock users at just $30 per month on both iOS and Android." While others are still debating AI safety, Musk is pushing boundaries. The speed of creation has dropped to under a minute. So the question is, will AI video replace creators or will you be building with it or watching from the sidelines? Bindu Ready just launched Deep Agent from abacus.ai, an AI agent that builds full applications for just $10 a month. It can create apps with payment processing faster than you can order lunch. With Deep Agent, you just type a prompt like build a subscription service with Stripe Payments and 22 minutes later, you're live with a custom domain. Entrepreneurs are already using it to create apps in under 30 minutes. One built a meditation app, another made a consulting platform, and a freelancer rebuilt a client portal during a launch. Deep Agent also works with multi-user systems, connects to tools like Slack, GitHub, and Salesforce, and automatically handles security features. For just $10 a month, you get 20,000 credits and three deep agent runs. $20 per month unlocks unlimited runs. Compare that to a $50,000 development quote. It's a no-brainer. You can try Deep Aagent today at deep aagent.abacus.ai. Get two free runs to test out complex tasks and start building apps right away. This is the future of app development. The question isn't whether AI will replace developers, it's whether you'll be building with it or watching from the sidelines. Google just solved AI's biggest problem, privacy. They've launched Embedding Gemma, a 308 million parameter AI that fits in under 200 megabytes smaller than a typical Netflix episode. and runs entirely offline. Full AI functionality all on your device without sending data to the cloud. Developers are using it offline for law firms, medical research and journalism. Law firms can search full case histories locally and medical apps can process sensitive patient data while staying compliant. Law firms can search full case histories locally and medical apps can process sensitive patient data while staying compliant. It integrates with sentence transformers, llama index, langchain, and cloudflare. Embedding Gemma topped the massive text embedding benchmark for Companies and developers can now run advanced AI locally, privately, and efficiently. It's available on HuggingFace, ready to integrate with Python and other frameworks. Ideogram just solved AI art consistency. Their style references feature learns any visual style from just three images and applies it across unlimited generations. Consistency is hard. Different designers interpret style guides differently, and AI often produces wildly different results. Upload three reference images, logos, pose, sketches, and idiogram locks in your colors, textures, typography, and composition. Every generated image matches perfectly. Agencies are already using it for hundreds of assets. Access it at ideoggram.ai or on iOS. Riplet just dropped agent 3 and it's not just an assistant. It's a full developer that works while you sleep. Test apps three times faster, 10 times cheaper, and run autonomously for over 200 minutes straight. It builds, tests, fixes, and deploys applications, all without human intervention. Two months ago, Replet's AI went rogue and deleted an entire production database. That disaster became the catalyst for Agent 3's new safety and testing systems. Agent 3 now spawns actual browser sessions and tests your app like a real QA engineer. It clicks buttons, fills out forms, checks APIs, verifies database rights, and tests edge cases. When it finds bugs, it fixes them and retests. CEO Amjad Masad called agent 3 the full self-driving moment of software. The evolution is remarkable. Agent one worked for 2 minutes, agent 2 for 20 minutes, and agent 3 runs over 200 minutes autonomously and working. The era of autonomous software development isn't coming. It's here.
